subject: Biology
title: Enzymes: Biological Catalysts
description: A quick, practical guide to Enzymes: Biological Catalysts.

hook.text: Chemical reactions in your body would take years without help. Enzymes finish them in milliseconds.

buildup.text: Each enzyme has a specific shape that fits one molecule—like a lock and key.

discovery.text: Enzymes lower the activation energy for reactions without being used up themselves.

twist.text: A single enzyme can process thousands of reactions per second without wearing out.

climax.text: Too hot or too acidic and enzymes unfold, losing their shape and function forever.

punchline.text: Life's speed depends on molecular matchmakers.

quiz.question: How do enzymes speed up reactions?
